article_meta.title: Venezuelan Political Crisis Venezuela releases multiple American citizens from prison following military operation At le

reasoning: No attribution violations, but two of three flagged items are weak or incorrect (numbers were attributed; broad context omission claim is overreach). Missed a clearer headline spin/causality implication. One remaining item’s label is debatable.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged text came from the author’s prose; no quoted or attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: The claim of missing attribution on prisoner counts is wrong (it’s attributed to the interim government/NGO via Bloomberg), and the broad ‘Bias by Omission’ about explaining the interim government is overreaching.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed a clearer potential bias in the headline implying causality (‘following military operation’).
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: For the one arguably valid item (U.S. military operation stated as fact), ‘Omission of Source Attribution’ would fit better than ‘Unsubstantiated Claims’, making the applied label debatable.
